description: RAG fixture set for SOLA embedding-only vs. two-stage rerank benchmark. 40 learner-style questions labeled against real BUS101 and POLSC101 chunk IDs from the dev.sylr.org index. Questions are authored to sound like real students: short, sometimes vague, occasional typos, not keyword queries.

fixtures.question: what even is a business? like why do we need them
fixtures.expected_substring: people outside of the business enterprise ought to have a say in how the business operates
fixtures.notes: Vague opener; should match the intro 'What Is a Business?' chunk

fixtures.question: does a company have to care about the environment or just make money
fixtures.expected_substring: environments that are free from sexual harassment and all types of discrimination
fixtures.notes: CSR question; should retrieve the Corporate Social Responsibility chunk

fixtures.question: what happens to workers when a coal plant closes down
fixtures.expected_substring: biomass electricity generation on the sites, to take advantage of existing transmission infrastructure
fixtures.notes: Direct topic reference; should match the 'When Coal Plants Shut Down' chunk

fixtures.question: how does GDP relate to how well a country is doing
fixtures.expected_substring: GDP per capita exactly equals the gross domestic income (GDI) per capita
fixtures.notes: Should retrieve the GDP/economic indicators chunk

fixtures.question: what does the Fed do exactly and how does it effect inflation
fixtures.expected_substring: monetary policy, the Fed relies on three tools: reserve requirements, the discount rate, and open market operations
fixtures.notes: Misspelling 'effect' for 'affect'; should retrieve Federal Reserve chunk

fixtures.question: why is it hard to sell products in other countries
fixtures.expected_substring: Distance is thus one of the natural barriers to international trade. Language is another natural trade barrier
fixtures.notes: Global trade barriers; should match the competing in global marketplace chunk

fixtures.question: what is a contract manufacturing arrangement
fixtures.expected_substring: volume of products to specification and put the domestic firm's brand name on the goods
fixtures.notes: Specific trade strategy; should match the global marketplace licensing/manufacturing chunk

fixtures.question: is the WTO the same as the EU? whats the difference
fixtures.expected_substring: renamed as such in 1993) was an international organization created by the 1957 Treaty of Rome
fixtures.notes: Confusion between trade organizations; should retrieve EU/global trade facilitators chunk

fixtures.question: what are the advantages of a sole proprietorship
fixtures.expected_substring: What is a sole proprietorship? Why is this a popular form of business organization? What are the drawbacks
fixtures.notes: Direct business law question; should match forms of business ownership chunk

fixtures.question: ben and jerry started their ice cream company together, what type of business is that
fixtures.expected_substring: cream business as a partnership was inexpensive and let them combine their limited financial resources
fixtures.notes: Scenario-based question; should retrieve the partnerships chunk with Ben and Jerry reference

fixtures.question: what makes small businesses important for the economy
fixtures.expected_substring: discourages start-ups and expansions, which decreases the workforce through layoffs
fixtures.notes: Should match the small businesses and US economy chunk

fixtures.question: groupon sounds like it lost alot of money, why would daily deals sites fail
fixtures.expected_substring: daily deals website isn't just unprofitable - it's bleeding hundreds of million
fixtures.notes: Slightly mangled question about Groupon failure case; should match starting a business chunk

fixtures.question: what should I think about before starting my own company
fixtures.expected_substring: first step in starting your own business is a self-assessment to determine whether you have the personal traits
fixtures.notes: Broad entrepreneurship question; should retrieve entrepreneurship getting-started chunk

fixtures.question: what goes in a business plan
fixtures.expected_substring: target market, pricing, distribution, and promotion
fixtures.notes: Basic business plan question; should retrieve the business plan components chunk

fixtures.question: how does e-commerce let companies collect customer data
fixtures.expected_substring: be used to collect data from both existing and potential customers
fixtures.notes: E-commerce data collection; should match the marketing/e-commerce chunk

fixtures.question: woody allen quote about innovation I think was in the reading
fixtures.expected_substring: Woody Allen once put it
fixtures.notes: Student remembers a specific quote; verbatim content match test

fixtures.question: what modes of transportation do companies use to ship goods
fixtures.expected_substring: Pipelines account for 3 percent of expenditures
fixtures.notes: Logistics/distribution question; should retrieve placing a product chunk

fixtures.question: how is SWOT analysis done with a real company
fixtures.expected_substring: SWOT analysis to various scenarios
fixtures.notes: Applied SWOT; should retrieve marketing segmentation/SWOT chunk

fixtures.question: whats the difference between managerial and financial accounting
fixtures.expected_substring: management accounting helps
fixtures.notes: Accounting distinction; should match the role of accounting in business chunk

fixtures.question: what does an income statement show me
fixtures.expected_substring: shows sales, expenses, and net p
fixtures.notes: Basic income statement question; should match income statement chunk

fixtures.question: how do you figure out if a company is financially healthy using ratios
fixtures.expected_substring: ratio analysis be used to identify a firm's financial strengths and weaknesses
fixtures.notes: Ratio analysis application; should retrieve financial ratio analysis chunk

fixtures.question: what tools does the federal reserve use for monetary policy
fixtures.expected_substring: three tools: reserve requirements, the discount rate, and open market operations
fixtures.notes: Fed tools question; two chunks mention this - chunk 466 vs 121 - tests ranking

fixtures.question: how does a company get a loan and what affects the interest rate
fixtures.expected_substring: quality of the collateral, and the debt-paying ability of the borrower
fixtures.notes: Business financing question; should retrieve financing options loan chunk

fixtures.question: what is the 401k and should I start one
fixtures.expected_substring: defined contribution retirement plan, is a form of savings plan
fixtures.notes: Personal finance question; should retrieve personal finances/401k chunk

fixtures.question: what is span of control in management
fixtures.expected_substring: frequency and type of interaction, proximity of subordinates, competence of both supervisor
fixtures.notes: Management concept; should retrieve the organizing/span of control chunk

fixtures.question: why would a company hire from outside instead of promoting internally
fixtures.expected_substring: Going outside gives you an opportunity to bring fresh ideas and skills into the company
fixtures.notes: HR recruiting question; should retrieve HRM external hiring chunk

fixtures.question: starbucks barista training how does that relate to HRM
fixtures.expected_substring: customer-service course, which trains you in making eye contact
fixtures.notes: Scenario recall; should match the Starbucks/coffee training anecdote in HRM chunk

fixtures.question: what does a performance review usually measure
fixtures.expected_substring: sometimes employers lay off workers because revenues are down
fixtures.notes: HRM appraisal question; should retrieve the appraisal/layoffs chunk

fixtures.question: how does a profit sharing bonus plan work
fixtures.expected_substring: operating profit soars to 20 percent, the employee bonuses go up to 26 percent
fixtures.notes: Employee compensation incentive; should retrieve great workplace/bonuses chunk

fixtures.question: what is insider trading and is it illegal
fixtures.expected_substring: news becomes public, you sell all the stock you own in the company
fixtures.notes: Business ethics/securities law; should retrieve ethics insider trading chunk

fixtures.question: how is political science different from other social sciences
fixtures.expected_substring: Situate the academic discipline of political science within the broader field of social science
fixtures.notes: Foundational POLSC101 question; should retrieve unit 1 learning outcomes chunk

fixtures.question: is political science actually a science or just opinions
fixtures.expected_substring: whether a particular government is fully democratic or is more or less democratic when compared
fixtures.notes: Should retrieve the research in political science chunk

fixtures.question: what is the american dream and why do people believe in it
fixtures.expected_substring: belief that each American has the freedom to pursue a better life
fixtures.notes: Political culture question; should retrieve American political culture chunk

fixtures.question: can politicians just ignore what their voters want
fixtures.expected_substring: responsibility of each representative to explain to the people back home why he or she has cast a vote
fixtures.notes: Public opinion and representation; should retrieve public opinion chunk

fixtures.question: what are lobbyists and do they actually bribe politicians
fixtures.expected_substring: research on lobbying has progressed substantially
fixtures.notes: Interest groups and lobbying; should retrieve lobbying/special interests chunk

fixtures.question: why does the US only have two main parties when other countries have more
fixtures.expected_substring: parliamentary arrangement such as Britain
fixtures.notes: Two-party vs multiparty question; should retrieve two-party system chunk

fixtures.question: what is democratic socialism and how is it different from communism
fixtures.expected_substring: revolutionary vanguard party, and the achievement of a direct-democracy dictatorship of the proletariat
fixtures.notes: Ideological distinction; should retrieve democratic socialism chunk

fixtures.question: what did laissez faire actually mean in economics
fixtures.expected_substring: transactions between private parties are free from tariffs, government subsidies, and enforced monopolies
fixtures.notes: Classical liberalism definition; should retrieve laissez-faire chunk

fixtures.question: how was the united nations created and what is it supposed to do
fixtures.expected_substring: ratification of the Charter of the United Nations in October 1945
fixtures.notes: UN origins; should retrieve the United Nations chunk

fixtures.question: what is terrorism and how is it different from regular war
fixtures.expected_substring: deliberate commission of an act of violence to create an emotional response through the suffering
fixtures.notes: War and terrorism definition; should retrieve terrorism chunk
